name: Tarte Tatin
recipeIngredient: Vanilla bean, split and seeds scraped out
recipeInstructions:
1. For the pastry: Sift flour and salt into a bowl. Rub in butter until crumbly. Stir in sugar.
2. Mix egg with enough water to bind the ingredients. Combine with dry mixture.
3. Wrap dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
4. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
5. Butter four molds, baking pans or ramekins at least 1 inch deep.
6. Peel and core apples; cut into six equal segments.
7. To make the caramel: Melt sugar, water, and butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add vanilla bean seeds and cook until light caramel color forms.
8. Add apple segments to the caramel and cook for 2-3 minutes.
9. Add nuts (pecans, pine nuts, pistachios) separately from apples; cook for an additional 5 minutes.
10. Roll out dough into a circle about 1/8 inch thick. Using the bottom of a mold as a guide, cut four discs.
11. Bake pastry on a baking sheet at 375°F (190°C) for 7-10 minutes until golden.
12. Remove nuts from caramel apple mixture; arrange apple slices in molds, using most of the syrup.
13. Cover each tart with a pastry disc.
14. Lower oven temperature to 340°F (170°C).
15. Bake tarts at 340°F (170°C) for an additional 10-15 minutes.
16. Cool tarts and turn them upside down onto serving plates.
17. Top each tart with a spoonful of vanilla ice cream, and around the edges, add more caramel and nuts from the pan.
18. Makes 4 servings.
